Dr. Lynn Thomason

B.S. General Studies in Physical Sciences 1982, Washington State University.

Washington State Teaching Certificate 1984, Washington State University.

M.S. Chemistry 1987, University of Oregon.

Ph.D. Chemistry 1993, University of Oregon.

Thesis title: "Tests of in vivo packagability of bacteriophage lambda monomeric chromosomes in exonuclease-deficient Escherichia coli cells."

Postdoctoral studies: 1993-1997, University of Oregon; 1997-1998, University of Texas at Austin; 1998-2000, University of California at Berkeley.

Joined the Molecular Sciences Institute in May, 2000.

Collaborating with Drew Endy on building new, characterized biological systems and studying bacteriophage development using genomic tools.

Return to the Molecular Sciences Institute People Page
